secondary_archetype: Data / Analytics PM (SevenRooms / hospitality SaaS context)

level_signals: 4+ years of product management experience required
2+ years of experience building data products, analytics features, or AI/ML solutions
Comp range $159,800–$235,000 base, consistent with mid-to-senior IC PM

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of product management experience, ideally within a SaaS or B2C environment
must_haves.qualifier: ideally within a SaaS or B2C environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2+ years of experience building data products, analytics features, or AI/ML solutions, ideally within a SaaS environment
You have a strong ability to connect complex technical features directly to customer value and business outcomes (like ROI, adoption, and retention)
You have a track record of owning and delivering product features or initiatives end-to-end with measurable impact
You are comfortable operating independently on product execution while collaborating across teams
You are highly analytical and use data to inform decisions and measure success
You have experience working in Agile environments and partnering closely with Engineering and Design
You are an effective communicator who can align stakeholders and drive clarity across cross-functional teams
You are naturally curious, customer-focused, and continuously seek to improve products and processes

scope.surface: Reporting, analytics, and restaurant insights infrastructure for SevenRooms (hospitality SaaS platform)
core_responsibilities: Own the strategic roadmap for reporting, analytics, and restaurant insights infrastructure
Partner with engineering teams to unlock the ability to use data in new ways to help restaurants make better decisions
Own the end-to-end lifecycle of product features and initiatives, from problem definition through launch and iteration
Write clear product requirements and partner with Engineering and Design to deliver high-quality solutions
Contribute to product roadmap development by identifying opportunities, defining scope, and prioritizing work that aligns with team OKRs and business impact
Work closely with Engineering, Design, Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to align on priorities
Conduct user research, analyze customer feedback, and leverage data to deeply understand customer needs
Define success metrics, monitor product performance, and use insights to inform decisions
Manage timelines, anticipate risks, and navigate trade-offs between business needs and technical constraints

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and reasonably wide ($159,800–$235,000 base)
Equity grants mentioned as part of compensation
Comprehensive benefits package described (401k match, 16 weeks parental leave, wellness, medical/dental/vision)
Clear ownership area: data and insights infrastructure for SevenRooms
Collaborative team culture described with strong cross-functional emphasis
red_flags: Role title is 'Product Manager, In-Store' but the JD body describes a 'Data and Insights' PM at SevenRooms — significant mismatch suggesting the wrong JD body was associated with this posting
Location/work arrangement is completely unstated — no city, no remote/hybrid/onsite designation provided
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years of experience requirements)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'SaaS' and hospitality operator context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and requirement text; not explicitly listed as metrics)
ambiguities: Role title is 'Product Manager, In-Store' but the entire JD body describes a 'Product Manager focusing on Data and Insights' at SevenRooms — this is likely a content/template mismatch in the ATS. The structured extraction reflects the JD body content, not the title.
The JD is branded as DoorDash but the team described is SevenRooms (a DoorDash subsidiary/acquisition). It is unclear whether the employer of record is DoorDash or SevenRooms.
Location type cannot be determined — no city, office, or remote policy is mentioned in the JD body.
